data.description:
## What
One-time backfill of `close_class` for already-closed bugs from STRUCTURAL signals only: a bug closed via a REVERT -> `regression`; a bug linked to a plan-review BLOCK -> `plan_defect`; otherwise `undetermined`. No prose recovery, no LLM guessing.

## Files
- `scripts/backfill_bug_class.py` â€” new: classify closed bugs from event signals, write a labeled backfill record.
- `tests/unit/scripts/test_backfill_bug_class.py` â€” new.

## Approach
The original free-text reasons were discarded, so only structural signals are trustworthy; classifying solely from REVERT/BLOCK linkage keeps the backfill honest and avoids the classification contamination the research warns about. Backfilled classes carry `source=backfill_structural, confidence=classified` so the registry keeps them apart from going-forward `--class` values.

## Tests
- Unit (temp store): a bug closed after a REVERT is `regression`; a bug linked to a plan-review BLOCK is `plan_defect`; a plain close is `undetermined`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/unit/scripts/test_backfill_bug_class.py` passes: the three fixtures classify to `regression`, `plan_defect`, `undetermined` respectively (exact-value asserts).
- [ ] every backfilled record carries `source` and `confidence=classified` â€” test asserts the label on output.
data.title: Backfill historical bug --class from structural signals only
